free_text: Energy Dependence of the 208Pb(4He,t) and (4He,3He)
Reaction from 10 to 20 MeV/Nucleon
The outgoing particles were
detected by two telescopes with a fixed separation
angle of approximately 6 degrees mounted on a
remotely controlled arm. Each consisted of a Si
deltaE detector (200 mu-m at 40 MeV and 500 mu-m at
the higher energies) followed by a 5 mm Si(Li) E
detector. The smaller angle telescope subtended 0.22
msr and the larger angle telescope, 0.36 msr. A 4 mm
Si(Li) detector subtending a solid angle of 0.2 msr
mounted on a second arm served as a monitor.
The target used was 1 mg/cm2 isotopically pure 208Pb
evaporated onto a 10 mu-g/cm2 12C backing.
Optical model analysis, DWBA analysis, deduced
spectroscopic factors.
OG
The statistical errors in the data range
from about 1-2% at the smallest angles to about 10% at
the largest angles.
No information on source of uncertainties,
presented in figure.
Figs.2-4
No information on source of uncertainties,
presented in figures.
